preFix.title: Pattern matching fabric on a drop-in seat
preFix.subtitle: Centre a fabric pattern on a drop-in chair seat so the repeat falls symmetrically before cutting
preFix.excerpt: How to measure the pattern repeat of an upholstery fabric, find the centre line of a drop-in seat pad, align the dominant motif symmetrically over the pad, mark the cut line with wrap allowance, and check for a consistent repeat across a matching set of chairs.
preFix.sourceNotes: The Complete Guide to Upholstery, Cherry Dobson. Upholstery Techniques and Projects, David James. Singer Sewing Reference Library.

content.text: Cutting a patterned fabric without first checking the
pattern repeat
will leave the
dominant motif
off-centre or sliced through on the finished
drop-in seat
. The cut is non-reversible, so plan the position on the fabric before shears are opened.
Step 1, measure the pattern repeat
Lay the fabric right side up on a flat surface. Identify the
dominant motif
. Measure from any point on that motif to the identical point on the next complete motif below: this distance is the
pattern repeat
. Note the measurement. For a half-drop repeat (where successive columns are offset vertically by half a repeat), also measure the horizontal repeat width.
Step 2, find the centre of the seat pad
Measure the width and depth of the
drop-in seat

board. Mark the midpoint of the front edge and the midpoint of the back edge, then draw a pencil line connecting them: this is the front-to-back centre line. Mark the left and right midpoints and connect them. The intersection of these two lines is the visual centre of the seat.
Step 3, position the pad over the fabric
Lay the fabric right side up. Position the seat pad face down on the fabric, aligning its centre mark over the centre of the nearest complete
dominant motif

. Flip the pad face up and check from above that the motif is centred and symmetrical across the width and depth of the pad. Adjust until the alignment is correct, then hold the pad in place with masking tape tabs at the corners while marking.
Step 4, mark and confirm the cut line

With the pad positioned on the wrong side of the fabric, add 100 mm to each edge as wrap allowance: this is sufficient to reach the underside of the board and staple. Mark the cut line with the pencil and combination square. Before cutting, check that the same portion of the
pattern repeat
appears on both the left and right wrap allowances: unequal allowances indicate the pad was positioned off-centre.
Step 5, cut and verify before stapling
Cut along the marked lines with the upholstery shears in a single continuous stroke per edge. Lay the cut panel right side up over the pad face and confirm the
dominant motif
is centred before stapling. Also confirm the grain (warp thread) runs straight from front to back: fabric pulled off-grain during stapling will distort the pattern.
Variations
A matching set of dining chairs requires the
pattern repeat

to fall in the same position on every seat. Mark all panels on the roll before cutting any. Use masking tape flags to note each position and confirm the repeat distance between panels is a whole multiple of the
pattern repeat
. On plain or small-texture fabrics, pattern matching does not apply. Cut on-grain and confirm the texture direction runs the same way across the whole set.

attrs.fix: Remove the staples and lift the fabric. Lay the cut panel back over the pad and measure from each edge of the pad to the nearest complete motif on both sides. If the imbalance is small, re-staple starting from the centre. If large, recut from fresh fabric.
attrs.cause: Pad shifted on the fabric between marking and cutting; or unequal wrap allowances on left and right.
attrs.symptom: Dominant motif appears off-centre after stapling

attrs.fix: Remove the fabric. Pull a single warp thread from the cut edge to confirm the grain direction, then realign the panel so the grain runs straight front-to-back before re-stapling. If the fabric has a diagonal print on a straight grain, the lean cannot be fully corrected.
attrs.cause: Fabric was cut or tensioned off-grain; the warp thread did not run parallel to the front-to-back centre line.
attrs.symptom: Pattern appears to lean diagonally across the seat surface after stapling

attrs.fix: For future sets, mark all panel positions on the roll before cutting any. Measure the repeat distance between each panel mark and confirm each falls at the same point within the repeat cycle.
attrs.cause: Each panel was positioned independently from a different starting point on the roll, without measuring the repeat distance between them.
attrs.symptom: Matching chairs in a set do not have the same motif position

initialFindings.message: TECHNIQUE tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
